title: lupinus lyallii var. macroflorus
intro: Lupinus lyallii var. macroflorus, also known as the tall lupine, is a variety of the lupine plant, specifically a flowering plant of the genus Lupinus. It is native to the mountainous regions of New Zealand and is known for its tall spikes of purple flowers, which attract various pollinators. The tall lupine has been found to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be used to treat wounds and skin conditions. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which offer potential health benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved cardiovascular health, and enhanced immune function.
